message:
docs(README): correct method count + soften 'all green' claim (br-wgth / fd90.75)

Continuation of fd90.74 README audit. Two more stale claims:

(1) Line 125 crate-tree comment: fp-frame "788 methods" → actual
    is 964 `pub fn` items (counted via grep). Updated to "960+
    methods" so the figure stays accurate as the crate continues
    to grow.

(2) Line 869 conformance-gate description: "all currently green
    under cargo test -p fp-conformance" — actually 3 of 170
    packet_filter tests fail (csv parse_dates mixed timezone, df
    concat axis=1, df to_json records orient) plus 2 harness-
    infrastructure tests (live oracle availability + JSON fuzz
    seed fixture). Total 5 known failures across the suite.

Soften to: "The vast majority pass under `cargo test -p
fp-conformance` (557+ passing); a small number of known mismatch
packets remain on the backlog (currently 3 packet filter cases on
time-zone parse_dates, axis-1 concat, and JSON records orient —
tracked as backlog beads)." This way users running the test
suite see expected behavior instead of "everything broken".

Pure README change. Build passes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -739,6 +739,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenpandas-wa26","title":"fd90.68: fp-groupby needs crate-level rustdoc for groupby aggregation engine","description":"Continuation of fd90.61-67 doc audit. Add crate-level //! rustdoc to fp-groupby - the standalone groupby aggregation engine. Public surface: GroupByOptions / GroupByExecutionOptions, GroupByError, AggFunc enum, groupby_sum / mean / count / min / max / first / last / std / var / median / nunique / prod / size aggregations, groupby_agg dispatcher, HyperLogLog approximate-distinct-count primitive. Pure docs. Verify cargo doc cleanly under RUSTDOCFLAGS=-D warnings.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-26T06:20:27.880362095Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T06:22:33.296973084Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T06:22:33.294410403Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","fd90","review"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-weh1","title":"[LOW] community: no .github/FUNDING.yml — no Sponsor button / funding channels declared","description":"GitHub Sponsors / funding configuration audit.

**Observation.** `ls .github/FUNDING.yml` → No such file. No declared funding channels (GitHub Sponsors / Open Collective / Liberapay / etc.).

**Context.** For an open-source library targeting a sizable user base (pandas has tens of millions of users; frankenpandas targets their Rust-curious subset), a documented funding mechanism is a cheap way to let engaged users contribute financially. GitHub renders a "Sponsor" button on the repo header when `FUNDING.yml` is present; without it, the button is absent and engaged users have no path.

**Severity.** LOW — no operational impact. No release blocker. No code quality issue. Filing truthfully as LOW; user-visible polish, optional feature.

**Fix shape.** `.github/FUNDING.yml`:
```yaml
# Map the repo's "Sponsor" button to one or more funding platforms.
# Each line enables a platform. Remove entries that don't apply.

github: [<org-or-user>]           # GitHub Sponsors
# open_collective: frankenpandas
# liberapay: <name>
# ko_fi: <name>
# custom: ["https://frankenpandas.dev/sponsor"]
```

Requires a decision: does the maintainer have an active GitHub Sponsors account? If yes, one-line file. If not, skip this bead entirely.

Blast radius: 1 tiny file (< 10 lines), zero code changes.

Prerequisites: Maintainer decides which funding channel(s) to expose. Nothing else.

Related:
- br-8k1i (SECURITY.md), br-6d5s (CONTRIBUTING.md) — same .github/ polish tier.

@@ -122,7 +122,7 @@ frankenpandas/
 │   ├── fp-types/         # Scalar, DType, NullKind, coercion rules
 │   ├── fp-columnar/      # Column, ValidityMask, vectorized kernels
 │   ├── fp-index/         # Index, MultiIndex, alignment planning
-│   ├── fp-frame/         # DataFrame, Series, Categorical, 788 methods
+│   ├── fp-frame/         # DataFrame, Series, Categorical, 960+ methods
 │   ├── fp-expr/          # Expression parser, eval()/query()
 │   ├── fp-groupby/       # GroupBy with 3 execution paths
 │   ├── fp-join/          # Inner/Left/Right/Outer/Cross/Asof joins
@@ -866,7 +866,7 @@ let merged = df1.merge_with_options(&df2, &["key"], JoinType::Inner,
 ./scripts/phase2c_gate_check.sh
 ```
 
-Regenerates conformance packet artifacts and fails closed if any parity report or gate is not green. 430+ packet suites spanning 1249 fixtures cover alignment, join, groupby, concat, filter, CSV, dtype, null semantics, resample, rolling, groupby rolling/resample, datetime accessors, string accessors, and more — all currently green under `cargo test -p fp-conformance`.
+Regenerates conformance packet artifacts and fails closed if any parity report or gate is not green. 430+ packet suites spanning 1249 fixtures cover alignment, join, groupby, concat, filter, CSV, dtype, null semantics, resample, rolling, groupby rolling/resample, datetime accessors, string accessors, and more. The vast majority pass under `cargo test -p fp-conformance` (557+ passing); a small number of known mismatch packets remain on the backlog (currently 3 packet filter cases on time-zone parse_dates, axis-1 concat, and JSON records orient — tracked as backlog beads).
 
 ## Missing Data Handling
